title: OpenAI's Plan To Merge Chatbots And Agents — With Greg Brockman
summary: Greg Brockman, OpenAI president and co-founder, discusses OpenAI's trajectory toward merging chatbots and agents into persistent agentic systems. He argues that compute scarcity will determine the AI race outcome, expects pricing to commoditize premium intelligence, and predicts the interface itself will eventually disappear as AI becomes a personal agent acting on users' behalf.
writeup: OpenAI president and co-founder Greg Brockman lays out a trajectory in which chatbots and agents converge into persistent agentic systems. He argues compute scarcity will decide the outcome of the AI race, and expects pricing to commoditize premium intelligence over time. He also predicts the interface itself will eventually disappear as AI shifts toward a personal agent acting on a user's behalf, tying together test-time compute, natural language interfaces, and multi-agent systems.
